task_completion.description: The agent must enable volume rendering in 3D Slicer to visualize the CT data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible. The 'Volume Rendering' module is active. The 'eye' icon next to the volume is toggled on, or a 3D volumetric rendering is visible in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: The agent must switch the rendering technique to Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Volume Rendering' module settings, the 'Technique' or 'Preset' dropdown indicates 'MIP' or 'Maximum Intensity Projection'. Alternatively, the 3D view displays a translucent, X-ray-like projection where internal high-density structures are visible, rather than a solid surface render.

task_completion.description: The 3D view must be oriented to a Coronal (front-to-back) projection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows the anatomy (spine and aorta) oriented vertically in the center of the frame, viewed from the front (Anterior-Posterior view).

task_completion.description: The visualization settings (Window/Level) must be adjusted so the aorta is clearly visible and bright.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The abdominal aorta (central vertical vessel running along the spine) appears bright and distinct. The image is not washed out (all white) or too dark (all black). The contrast allows differentiation between the vessel and surrounding soft tissue.

task_completion.description: The agent must create a JSON file documenting the visualization parameters used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., 'cat') displays the content of 'mip_parameters.json'. The content includes keys for 'window_width' (approx 400-880), 'window_level' (approx 150-300), and 'slab_thickness' (approx 100-150).

task_completion.description: The final screenshot and parameters file must be saved to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window or terminal 'ls' command shows 'aorta_mip.png' and 'mip_parameters.json' located in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent must use the 3D Slicer Graphical User Interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer application window with visible interaction (mouse cursor, highlighted buttons, open menus) rather than purely command-line manipulation or script execution.

integrity.description: The output image must be generated within the session, not opened from an existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen capturing the screen or saving the view from Slicer, rather than opening an image viewer to display a pre-existing 'aorta_mip.png'.

integrity.description: The parameters in the JSON file should reflect the settings used in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values entered into the JSON file (Window/Level) are consistent with the values visible in the Slicer 'Volume Rendering' or 'Window/Level' settings panels during the session.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified clinical guidelines for CT Angiography/MIP recommend a Window Width between 400 and 880 HU and a Window Level between 150 and 300 HU. The synthetic dataset has an aorta density of 150-300 HU. Correct visualization should show the aorta as a bright, high-intensity structure.
